createdPhoto by Kevin Ku on PexelsHello, and welcome back!
This is a new article from the C++ in Practice series, where we will work through C++ idioms and design decisions using a small 2D game engine I’ve been building from scratch on top of the SDL library.
At first glance, the PImpl idiom may seem like a purely theoretical technique with limited practical value. But once we understand the problem it was designed to solve, it becomes one of the most elegant tools we as developers can use.
The PImpl concept is simple and straightforward to implement, but the correct implementation (especially with a smart pointer) brings together several C++ language rules that all collide in a single small class. So I’ve decided to split the topic into two parts: this article covers what PImpl is, the real problem it solves in our game engine project, and some trade-offs. The next part gets deep into the std::unique_ptr implementation and the language rules that come with using it.
If you're not familiar with the game engine project I am talking about, you can read more about it here.
A real problem: SDL is leaking through my header
